name: Cova del Boc
starting_point: Kilometer 4.2 of the Colonya Valley road
equipment: No vertical progression equipment needed. Helmet and lighting only.
description: A beautifully decorated baroque cave in Pollensa with a very small entrance barely one meter high. Once inside, the cave opens up to reveal abundant and varied speleothems including numerous curtains and handsome columns. The cave descends continuously, starting with low-ceiling passages requiring crawling before opening to allow upright walking.
full_description:
The entrance to the Cova del Boc is very small, not exceeding one meter in height and approximately one meter seventy in width, so it is difficult to see until we are right at the entrance. However, just a few meters past the entrance, a baroque cave loaded with decorations immediately opens before us, dazzling us with its beauty.

From the very entrance, the cave begins to delve into the depths of the earth in continuous descent, requiring us to move on all fours or in any other manner, since in this first part the cave has very little headroom. However, as our descent continues, the cave becomes larger so that we can walk upright while all manner of formations, each more beautiful than the last, present themselves before our eyes on all sides.

It is not that the cave at this point is a chamber where everything is visible before our eyes, since especially on its left side, winding passages appear that we can explore while also navigating the various obstacles that present themselves. Although the cave floor in some areas is not too wet, one must nevertheless be careful of slipping.

As I said at the beginning, the Cova del Boc is heavily loaded with decorations, having a great variety among which numerous curtains stand out, in addition to some beautiful columns.

It is impossible for me to explain the entirety of the cave since due to lack of time I could not reach its end, having to turn back at a point where the cave once again began to reduce in size, and after crawling for a stretch through it, I finally turned around, postponing a new visit.
